title: Compiler may optimize away memset() of rx_link_key in lichen_l2_input()
description:
In lichen_l2.c:496 and lichen_l2.c:507, memset(rx_link_key, 0, sizeof(rx_link_key)) is used to clear a local copy of the link encryption key. This memset may be optimized away by the compiler because 'rx_link_key' is a local variable that is never read after the memset.

Locations:
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:496 (error path)
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:507 (success path)

Impact: The 16-byte AES link key remains in stack memory after lichen_l2_input() returns. This is actual key material used for AES-CCM-64 MIC computation. If an attacker can read process memory (via a separate vulnerability, debugging interface, or crash dump), they can recover the link key and forge/decrypt link-layer frames.

Fix: Use explicit_bzero(), memset_s(), or Zephyr's secure_memzero() if available.
close_reason: Replaced memset() with secure_zero() for rx_link_key at lines 527 and 538 in lichen_l2.c. Added secure_zero helper with volatile pointer to prevent compiler optimization.
